exemplar.title:
What is the effect of script-based knowledge 
on memory recall of text?

detailedExemplarData.title:
What is the effect of script-based knowledge 
on memory recall of text?

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Clear, focused aim explicitly linked to Schema Theoryâs reconstructive memory claim
Concise yet accurate description of Schema Theory with appropriate citations
Independent and dependent variables correctly identified and operationalized in both directional and null hypotheses
weaknesses: Relevance discussion is somewhat brief and could be expanded with more contextual detail
Alternative theoretical perspectives are not considered
Minor imprecision in wording of the hypothesis could be tightened
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three introduction strands meet the 5â6 band descriptors: the aim is clear and its relevance explained; theory is accurately described and linked; variables are operationalized. Slight brevity in rationale and minor wording imprecision prevent a perfect 6, so a score of 5 reflects strong but not flawless performance.

strengths: Research design justified: counter-balancing and control of individual differences
Controlled variables identified and their impact on internal validity explained
Materials section explains why and how scripts were created and clustered
weaknesses: Sampling rationale is described but could more deeply analyse how participant demographics affect schema activation
Procedure clarity would benefit from a timeline or flowchart
Justification for material selection could be expanded to address script distinctiveness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The three exploration strands meet the 3â4 band descriptors: the repeated-measures design and controls are explained (4), sampling is described with partial justification (borderline 3â4) and controlled variables/materials are explained (4). Overall coherence and adequate depth warrant the full 4.

strengths: Appropriate use of descriptive statistics and repeated-measures ANOVA with post-hoc tests
Graphical presentation addresses the hypothesis with correct techniques
Interpretation links findings to hypothesis and reports effect size
weaknesses: Statistical reporting contains errors (incorrect degrees of freedom) undermining accuracy
Error-bar interpretation lacks clarity without mention of confidence intervals
Narrative lacks precise statistic values that would strengthen interpretation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Statistical techniques are appropriate but marred by reporting errors, placing this strand in the 3â4 band at the lower end (3). Although graphing and interpretation are strong, the analysisâs overall accuracy issues justify a conservative score of 3.

strengths: Strengths and limitations are stated, explained and relevant to validity and generalisability
Findings are related to background theory
weaknesses: Discussion of findings is descriptive and lacks nuanced engagement with alternative perspectives
Modification justifications are brief and sometimes generic
Depth of theoretical integration could be expanded
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Evaluation strands show described findings with reference to theory (3), well-explained strengths/limitations (5) and linked modifications (3). Mixed depth across strands places performance in the mid 3â4 band; a score of 3 reflects solid but unsophisticated evaluation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The bar graph displays mean unstated-action recall for each IV level with error bars, a labelled x-axis (number of script versions) and a y-axis (number of actions).  It directly visualises the tested relationship and includes an appropriate title.  Minor stylistic issues (lower-case labels) do not detract from correctness; therefore the strand meets the 5-6 descriptor, warranting 5 marks.

The student clearly states the statistical outcome (significant ANOVA, effect size), interprets what this means for the data, and links the findings back to the directional hypothesis by explicitly accepting Hâ and rejecting Hâ.  The interpretation is coherent and grounded in the reported statistics, fulfilling the 5-6 descriptor; limited mention of confidence intervals prevents awarding a 6, so 5 is given.

Both IV and DV are identified and operationalised: IV = number of script versions read (one, two or three); DV = count of unstated script actions recalled as scored in the written recall.  Directional and null hypotheses are clearly written.  Operational details such as the specific levels of the IV and the scoring procedure constitute adequate operationalisation, fulfilling the top-band descriptor; minor wording imprecision ("number of texts") prevents maximal precision, so 5 is awarded.

Convenience sampling is described and partially justified as practical for a school study.  Participant characteristics (age, language, exclusion of psychology students) are provided.  However, the rationale is brief and does not fully analyse how these features serve the study aims, so the work meetsâbut does not exceedâthe explanation threshold; the lower mark (3) in the 3-4 band is appropriate.

Schema Theory is described with key concepts (schemas, scripts, reconstructive memory) and supporting citations (Bartlett, Popov et al.).  The student then links the theory to the present investigation by explaining that script-consistent but unstated actions are expected to intrude during recall because memory is reconstructed from prior knowledge.  The connection is explicit and correct; only limited depth on alternative theoretical interpretations keeps the evidence at 5 rather than 6.

The discussion restates that results mirror Bower et al. and support Schema Theory, but depth is limited to a brief confirmation rather than a nuanced exploration of mechanisms or contrasting evidence.  The strand therefore meets the descriptor for describing findings with reference to theory (3-4 band) but not the richer âdiscussionâ expected for 5-6; 3 marks are appropriate.

The student states a clear aim â to examine whether exposure to increasing numbers of script-based stories leads to the recall of unstated script actions â and explicitly links this aim to the need to replicate and test Schema Theoryâs claim about reconstructive memory.  The paragraph that precedes the aim explains why studying script intrusions is relevant for demonstrating how schemas distort recall, thereby providing an explicit rationale.  Although the explanation is concise, it is accurate and sufficiently elaborated to satisfy the descriptor for the top band; a minor brevity in the relevance discussion justifies awarding the lower mark (5) within the 5-6 range.

Across sections 4.1 to 4.3 the student lists multiple strengths and limitations (e.g., repeated measures controls, demand characteristics, sampling bias) and explains how each impacts validity or generalisability.  Relevance to the present investigation is explicit, meeting the top-band descriptor.  Occasional repetition prevents full sophistication, so 5 rather than 6 is awarded.

Controlled variables (environment, language, standardised instructions, individual predispositions) are each named and their importance for internal validity is briefly explained.  The materials section justifies the creation, translation and clustering of stories, demonstrating understanding of how material choice underpins the manipulation.  This level of justification corresponds to the explanation band, earning the upper mark of 4.

Suggested improvementsârandom sampling for better generalisability, larger sample to stabilise effect size, deception to reduce demand characteristicsâare present and connected to stated limitations, but justification is brief and sometimes generic.  The work satisfies the descriptor for linkage yet lacks the detailed rationale required for the highest band, earning 3 marks.

The student not only states that a repeated-measures design was used but also explains why it controls individual differences and suits a small sample, and how counter-balancing was employed.  This explicit justification moves beyond description into explanation, fully matching the 3-4 band; the account is accurate and coherent, meriting the upper mark of 4 within that band.

Appropriate descriptive statistics (means, SDs) and an inferential test (repeated-measures ANOVA with Bonferroni post-hoc) are applied, showing awareness of the within-subjects design.  Nonetheless, the reported degrees of freedom (2,4) and very small p-values for 18 participants indicate computational or reporting errors, so accuracy is compromised.  This aligns with the 3-4 bandâcorrect techniques but with errorsâand the lower mark (3) is assigned.

annotations.comment: References follow APA style but check consistency in journal title formatting (italicization and capitalization).
The student offers a concise but thorough description of Schema Theory, outlining schemas, scripts and reconstructive memory with accurate citations. This establishes a solid theoretical foundation.
The summary of Bower et al. (1979) is clear but could more explicitly link this studyâs procedures to the present investigationâs aim, reinforcing the rationale for replication.
The aim is stated clearly and its relevance to Schema Theory is explained, providing a coherent justification for the experimentâs focus on script intrusions.
While the DV is operationalized, consider clarifying interârater reliability for scoring unstated actions to strengthen measurement validity.
Both null and directional hypotheses are correctly stated, with explicit reference to IV levels and predicted DV outcomes.
Justifies repeatedâmeasures design clearly, linking control of individual differences to internal validityâa strong explanation of the chosen design.
Convenience sampling is described, but a deeper justification of how participant demographics might influence schema activation would strengthen the sampling rationale.
The procedure is detailed but would benefit from a timeline table or flowchart to clarify session timings and tasks.
Controlled variables are named and their impact on validity is explained, demonstrating thorough consideration of factors that could confound recall.
Independent and dependent variables are correctly identified and operationalized, supporting clear measurement of the effect of text exposure on scriptâaction recall.
The materials section describes story clusters well. To deepen justification, explain how these specific scripts were chosen to maximize script distinctiveness.
Reporting F(2, 4) appears incorrect given N=18; degrees of freedom should be recalculated to reflect 17 df within groups.
The discussion links findings to the directional hypothesis and reports effect size clearly, demonstrating accurate interpretation.
Explicitly stating acceptance of Hâ and rejection of Hâ shows clear linkage between statistical outcome and hypothesis decision.
The evaluation restates support for Schema Theory but would benefit from engaging with alternative theoretical perspectives or contradictory findings.
Descriptive statistics are stated, but adding justification for using mean and SD over median or range in skewed data would strengthen analysis rigor.
The table presents means and SDs clearly, but column headings could specify units and clarify that percentages refer to % of total actions.
Error bars are included in the bar graph, but their interpretation is unclear without a legend or mention of confidence intervals.
Noting the moderate SDs as evidence of consistency is effective; linking this to each script type would further contextualize variability.
The narrative description of Table 1 and Figure 1 lacks specific statistics; integrating precise mean differences or effect sizes would strengthen interpretation.
Identifies opportunity sampling as timeâefficient, demonstrating an understanding of pragmatic strengths in schoolâbased research.
Acknowledges limited generalizability due to narrow sample; consider suggesting stratified random sampling to enhance representativeness.
Highlights control of confounds in materials and procedures, indicating robust internal validity safeguards.
Potential influence of existing participant schemas is noted; consider including a preâtest to gauge baseline script familiarity.
Acknowledge researcher triangulation effectively reduces bias; linking this to interârater reliability metrics would further strengthen the claim.
